title: lithocarpus orbicarpus
intro: Lithocarpus orbicarpus, also known as the Bur oak or Tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree that is native to parts of Asia, including China, Taiwan, and the Philippines. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus orbicarpus include its ability to treat a range of ailments, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Lithocarpus orbicarpus is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
